signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ Hawkish Shift
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -15,435 (specs fading hawkishness) vs RBNZ Governor Breman signaling the cash rate must rise further due to building inflationary pressures. With the RBNZ bias shifting toward a hike while specs remain net short, a significant short-squeeze risk is active. NZD/USD at 0.5910 faces immediate upside pressure if the committee formalizes this hawkish pivot.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hawkishness
signals.text: NZD outperformance (avg +0.604%) vs CAD weakness (avg -0.398%) creates a 1.002% G8 spread, the widest bilateral theme in the complex. The divergence is fundamentally driven by RBNZ Governor Breman's hike signals contrasting with a neutral BoC (2.25%). Despite the 1.002% spread, NZD faces a -75bp real rate deficit vs CAD (+0.18% vs -0.83%), suggesting the move is driven by policy delta rather than current carry.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Carry
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +57,180 (74.5% long) represents a crowded institutional long, while retail sentiment (68% short) provides a contrarian divergence. Options markets are pricing extreme caution with a +8.6% Vol Risk Premium (IV 16.7% vs HV30 8.1%) and a -0.70 25d RR put skew. The 60bp AUD carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%) supports the pair at 0.7133, but the high VRP suggests the market is paying heavily for downside protection against a positioning unwind.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Fed Rhetoric + Carry + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.55 remains supported by a +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%). Fed Governor Cook's warning of possible rate hikes if inflation worsens reinforces the USD bid, while the 25d RR at -1.34 (put skew) confirms the options market is actively hedging for BoJ intervention as price tests the 160.00 threshold. JPY COT LF net short -81,624 indicates a crowded carry trade vulnerable to verbal intervention.

signals.title: DXY vs SPX — Correlation Break + USD Funding
signals.text: The correlation between DXY and S&P 500 has shifted to -0.643 vs a 252d norm of -0.333 (z=+1.81σ). This significant deviation indicates an intensified inverse relationship where USD is acting as a primary funding stress indicator. With VIX at 16.3 (normal volatility), the USD bid is currently driven by Fed hawkishness (Jefferson/Cook) rather than broad risk-off, but the tightening correlation increases DXY sensitivity to any equity drawdown.
